behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: local function seam (template-lint rule over prompt text). MOCK-MISS: failure is in lint logic itself, not module handoff. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none required. Rejection-gate: Integration+ is disqualified by behavior text "static prompt-template lint check"; no real harness/runtime dependency is required.
behaviors.test_description: Call the lint rule on template strings containing known phase-instruction snippets copied from SKILL.md and assert rejection with precise diagnostics; call it on clean templates and assert pass.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation/fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam (template + interpolation + fixture assembly pipeline). MOCK-MISS: unit-testing only template text can miss leakage introduced during assembly. REAL-INFRA: no required OS/network/subprocess beyond in-process fixture wiring. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not needed. Rejection-gate: System is disqualified because behavior specifies harness assembly correctness, not real network/subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt-construction path used by tests (including fixture interpolation), then assert the final prompt text does not contain phase-specific instruction fragments from SKILL.md.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from the plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam with filesystem-backed context loading (harness + skill loader + evaluator). MOCK-MISS: hard-coded prompt paths can pass while file-based loading is broken. REAL-INFRA: local filesystem access fits Integration per level definitions; no external OS/network/subprocess requirement. LLM-DEP: this behavior is about provenance of context, not model quality. Rejection-gate: Agentic is disqualified because correctness criterion is source-of-instructions wiring, not stochastic model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the real test entrypoint with actual plugin/skill files present, and assert that loaded context references file-derived content (not embedded phase rules) and drives validation path selection.

behaviors.description: If the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails with the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: model-judgment seam (behavioral outcome must change when skill context quality regresses). MOCK-HIDE/LLM-DEP: deterministic stubs can still satisfy expected schema and would hide real regression in model behavior sensitivity to context. MULTI-STEP: single model evaluation, so not Workflow. Rejection-gate: Integration is disqualified by behavior text "behavioral test fails" under degraded SKILL.md using same prompt, which requires real model output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Run A/B executions with identical user-style prompt: baseline SKILL.md vs intentionally degraded/removed section. Assert the behavioral test passes baseline and fails degraded variant based on the same structured criteria.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam plus local artifact write ordering (model call wrapper -> artifact logger -> parser). MOCK-MISS: isolated parser/logger unit tests can miss ordering mistakes in orchestration. REAL-INFRA: local filesystem artifact write is sufficient; no need for real network/subprocess if model output can be stubbed at interface. Rejection-gate: System is disqualified because failure is ordering in local pipeline, not external infrastructure behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the invocation pipeline with a controlled model response; assert diagnostic artifact contains untouched raw output and is written before parser/extractor is invoked (e.g., via call-order hooks or timestamps).

behaviors.description: Assertions verify structured behavior (for example field placement or schema shape), not mere keyword presence that could come from prompt echoing.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: local assertion-function seam (evaluation criteria robustness). MOCK-MISS: failure is in assertion semantics and can be exposed with crafted outputs. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none required to prove keyword-echo false positives are rejected. Rejection-gate: Integration+ is disqualified because behavior text focuses on assertion form (schema/field placement) rather than runtime wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Feed the assertion logic crafted outputs: (1) keyword-present but wrong schema/field placement, expected fail; (2) correctly structured output without redundant keywords, expected pass.
